events.title: Joe Crookston
events.description: Joe Crookston is a dynamic Americana singer-songwriter and fiddle player who has toured with Gordon Lightfoot, headlined major US festivals, and received Folk Alliance International Album of the Year honors. Known for his infectious rhythm, poignant lyrics, and ability to create moments of transcendence on stage, he weaves together lap slide songs and American Southern tunes that draw audiences in.

events.description: A folk-rock band from the Southeast whose name reflects the tension between past and future. Time Sawyer's music addresses social divisions while fostering community connection, having performed at major festivals like MerleFest and Bristol Rhythm and Roots Reunion.

events.title: Windborne
events.description: Windborne is a vocal ensemble known for innovative arrangements of traditional songs from around the world, blending deep harmonic traditions with contemporary relevance. With 20 years of polyphonic music study among its members, the group creates emotionally resonant performances spanning continents and centuries while maintaining folk music's commitment to social activism.

events.title: Future IX Family Watch Party
events.description:
Join us on Thursday June 25th at Tall Ship Boston for a full day of family‑friendly waterfront futbol fun!

From 3PM–8PM, Tall Ship transforms into an interactive soccer experience for kids and families featuring training, games, skill challenges, giveaways, and activities for all ages.

past_events.title: The Heart Collectors
past_events.description: The Heart Collectors are a Celtic-influenced folk band merging 60's and 70's Americana with ethereal soundscapes, featuring four-part vocal harmonies and instruments including cello, banjo, and mandolin. Finalists at the International Folk Music Awards 2025 for Album of the Year, they've built international momentum performing over 100 shows annually across major music festivals.

past_events.title: Lena Fjortoft, Grace Givertz, and Jasper Jones
past_events.performer: Lena Fjortoft, Grace Givertz, and Jasper Jones
past_events.description: An evening featuring three indie folk and Americana artists: Lena Fjortoft, whose music blends Americana and alt-folk with ethereal vocals; Grace Givertz, an indie folk singer-songwriter from Florida known for heartfelt storytelling; and Jasper Jones, a Brooklyn-based non-binary artist whose debut EP explores themes of love and shadow.

past_events.title: Windborne
past_events.description: Windborne is a vocal ensemble known for innovative arrangements of traditional songs that blend polyphonic vocal harmony with contemporary interpretation. Drawing on 20 years of study in polyphonic music worldwide, the four singers deliver emotionally resonant performances spanning continents and centuries while honoring folk music's tradition of social activism.
